Authenticate and interact with Hot Metal programmatically.

# API Overview

The Hot Metal API gives you programmatic access to the platform. You can use it to build AI agent workflows, third-party integrations, and custom automation pipelines that create publications, generate drafts, publish posts, and more -- all without touching the dashboard.

## Base URL

All API endpoints are served under a single base URL:

```
https://app.hotmetalapp.com/agents-api/v1
```

Every path in this documentation is relative to that base. For example, `GET /me` means `GET https://app.hotmetalapp.com/agents-api/v1/me`.

## Authentication

Every request must include a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header. API keys start with the `hm_` prefix and are tied to your user account.

```
Authorization: Bearer hm_your_api_key_here
```

You can create and manage API keys from **Settings > API Keys** in the Hot Metal dashboard. Treat your API key like a password -- do not commit it to source control or share it publicly.

If the key is missing or invalid, the API returns a `401 Unauthorized` response.

## Request format

- Send request bodies as JSON with the `Content-Type: application/json` header.
- Use standard HTTP methods: `GET` to read, `POST` to create or trigger actions, `PATCH` to update, and `DELETE` to remove.
- Path parameters are part of the URL (e.g., `/publications/:id`). Query parameters go in the query string (e.g., `?status=new`).

## Response format

All successful responses wrap the result in a `data` envelope:

```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "b3f1a2c4-...",
    "name": "My Publication"
  }
}
```

List endpoints return an array inside `data`:

```json
{
  "data": [
    { "id": "b3f1a2c4-...", "name": "My Publication" },
    { "id": "e7d9f0a1-...", "name": "Another Publication" }
  ]
}
```

Error responses use a consistent shape with a human-readable message and a machine-readable code:

```json
{
  "error": "Publication not found",
  "code": "NOT_FOUND"
}
```

Quota errors include additional fields to help you understand usage limits:

```json
{
  "error": "Free plan allows up to 2 publications",
  "code": "QUOTA_EXCEEDED",
  "limit": 2,
  "current": 2,
  "upgradeEmail": "hello@hotmetalapp.com"
}
```

## HTTP status codes

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `200 OK` | Request succeeded. Response body contains the result. |
| `201 Created` | Resource was created successfully. |
| `202 Accepted` | Request accepted for asynchronous processing. The result will be delivered via webhook. |
| `400 Bad Request` | Validation failed -- check the `error` message for details. |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Missing or invalid API key. |
| `403 Forbidden` | You do not have permission, or a quota limit has been reached. |
| `404 Not Found` | The requested resource does not exist or does not belong to you. |
| `429 Rate Limited` | Too many requests. Back off and retry after a short delay. |
| `500 Internal Server Error` | Something went wrong on our end. |
| `502 Bad Gateway` | An upstream service returned an error. |
| `503 Service Unavailable` | A required service is not configured or reachable. |

## Error codes

Every error response includes a `code` field you can use for programmatic handling:

| Code | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `NOT_FOUND` | Resource does not exist or is not accessible. |
| `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Request body or parameters failed validation. |
| `FORBIDDEN` | Action not allowed for your account. |
| `QUOTA_EXCEEDED` | You have reached a plan limit. |
| `INVALID_JSON` | The request body is not valid JSON. |
| `GENERATION_FAILED` | The AI writer failed to produce a draft. |
| `PUBLISH_FAILED` | Publishing the draft to the CMS failed. |
| `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE` | A backend service is not configured or reachable. |
| `BAD_GATEWAY` | An upstream service returned an error. |
| `INTERNAL_ERROR` | An unexpected server-side error occurred. |

## Webhooks

Some endpoints accept an optional `webhookUrl` parameter to enable asynchronous processing. When you provide a webhook URL:

1. The API returns `202 Accepted` immediately with a session ID.
2. Processing happens in the background.
3. When the work completes (or fails), a `POST` request is sent to your webhook URL with the result.

Webhook payloads are JSON objects with this structure:

```json
{
  "event": "draft.ready",
  "sessionId": "a1b2c3d4-...",
  "publicationId": "e5f6g7h8-...",
  "data": { ... },
  "timestamp": "2026-03-13T15:30:00.000Z"
}
```

Possible event types:

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `draft.ready` | A draft was generated and is ready for review. |
| `draft.published` | A draft was generated and auto-published. |
| `draft.failed` | Draft generation failed. The `error` field contains details. |
| `scout.completed` | A content scout run finished. |

### Webhook security

Every webhook delivery is signed with HMAC-SHA256. The signature is sent in the `X-HotMetal-Signature` header as a hex string. To verify a webhook:

1. Read the raw request body as a string.
2. Compute the HMAC-SHA256 of the body using your API key as the secret.
3. Compare the result to the value in `X-HotMetal-Signature` using a timing-safe comparison.

Requests come from the `HotMetal-Webhook/1.0` user agent. If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status, Hot Metal retries the delivery up to 3 times with increasing delays.

### Webhook URL requirements

- Must use HTTPS.
- Must not point to localhost, private IP addresses (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x), or internal hostnames (.local, .internal).
- Maximum URL length is 2048 characters.

## CORS

The API allows requests from all origins, so you can call it from browser-based applications.

## Common patterns

- **IDs** are UUID strings (e.g., `"b3f1a2c4-5d6e-7f8a-9b0c-1d2e3f4a5b6c"`).
- **Timestamps** are Unix timestamps (integers) in most resource fields, and ISO 8601 strings in webhook payloads.
- **Nullable fields** are returned as `null` when not set.
- **List endpoints** return arrays directly in `data` without pagination cursors. Results are ordered by creation time.
- **Delete endpoints** return `{ "data": { "deleted": true } }` on success.
- **Ownership** is enforced automatically. You can only access resources that belong to your account.
Review, promote, or dismiss ideas to manage your content pipeline.

# Idea Actions

When you open an idea from the Ideas page, you land on the idea detail page. This is where you get the full picture and decide what to do with the idea.

## What you see on the detail page

The detail page gives you everything the Content Scout gathered for this idea:

- **Title** -- The proposed headline, displayed prominently at the top.
- **Status badge** -- The idea's current status (New, Reviewed, Promoted, or Dismissed), shown as a color-coded label.
- **Relevance score** -- A percentage indicating how closely the idea matches your topics.
- **Angle** -- The unique take or hook that makes this idea worth writing about.
- **Summary** -- A detailed breakdown of what the post could cover, the key points to address, and why the topic matters to your audience.
- **Sources** -- Each source the Scout referenced is listed with its title, a snippet of relevant text, and a link to the original article. Click any source to open it in a new tab.
- **Metadata** -- The creation date and last-updated date for the idea.

## Status flow

Ideas follow a simple lifecycle:

**New** → **Reviewed** → **Promoted** or **Dismissed**

Every idea starts as "New" when the Scout generates it. From there, you move it forward based on your decision.

## Mark Reviewed

When an idea is in the "New" state, you will see a **Mark Reviewed** button. Clicking it changes the status to "Reviewed," which signals that you have read and considered the idea. This is useful for keeping track of what you have already looked at, especially when the Scout generates a large batch of ideas at once.

Marking an idea as reviewed also clears it from the "new ideas" notification count in the sidebar.

## Promote to Writing Session

This is the main action for ideas you want to turn into published posts. Click **Promote to Writing Session** to:

1. Create a new writing session that is pre-loaded with the idea's title, summary, and source links.
2. Update the idea's status to "Promoted."
3. Navigate you directly to the new writing session.

Once you land in the writing session, the AI writing partner already has the idea's full context and will begin drafting based on the title, angle, summary, and sources. You can guide the draft from there through conversation.

The Promote button is available on ideas with a "New" or "Reviewed" status. If an idea has already been promoted or dismissed, you won't see it.

## Go to Writing Session

After an idea has been promoted, the Promote button is replaced by a **Go to Writing Session** button. Click it to jump straight to the writing workspace where the AI is drafting (or has drafted) the post based on this idea. This makes it easy to revisit promoted ideas and pick up where you left off.

## Dismiss

If an idea doesn't fit your plans, click **Dismiss** to remove it from your active pipeline. The idea's status changes to "Dismissed," and it moves out of your main workflow.

You can dismiss an idea from either the "New" or "Reviewed" state. Once an idea has been promoted to a writing session, it can no longer be dismissed -- the writing session is already underway.

Dismissed ideas aren't deleted. You can still find them by filtering for "Dismissed" on the Ideas page if you ever want to revisit them.

## Next steps

- [Browse and filter ideas](/content-scout/browsing-ideas) to learn how to find the right ideas quickly.
- [Read how the Scout works](/content-scout/how-it-works) to understand where these ideas come from.
Filter, review, and manage ideas generated by the Content Scout.

# Browsing Ideas

The Ideas page is your central hub for everything the Content Scout discovers. Every idea across all your publications lands here, and you have full control over how you filter, sort, and manage them.

## Getting to the Ideas page

Click **Ideas** in the sidebar to open the Ideas page. If the Scout has found new ideas since your last visit, you will see a notification badge on the sidebar icon letting you know there are fresh ideas to review.

## Filtering by publication

By default, the Ideas page shows ideas from all your publications combined, sorted by newest first. To narrow the view down to a single publication, use the publication dropdown at the top of the page. Select a specific publication to see only ideas generated for that publication's topics.

## Filtering by status

Below the publication dropdown, you will find a row of status filter buttons. Use these to focus on ideas at a particular stage:

- **All** -- Shows every idea regardless of status.
- **New** -- Ideas the Scout just generated that you haven't looked at yet.
- **Reviewed** -- Ideas you have marked as reviewed but haven't promoted or dismissed.
- **Promoted** -- Ideas you have promoted into writing sessions.
- **Dismissed** -- Ideas you have decided to skip.

## Reading idea cards

Each idea appears as a card with the following information at a glance:

- **Title** -- The proposed headline for the post.
- **Angle preview** -- The first couple of lines of the idea's unique angle, so you can quickly gauge whether it interests you.
- **Status badge** -- A color-coded label showing the idea's current status (New, Reviewed, Promoted, or Dismissed).
- **Relevance score** -- A percentage indicating how closely the idea aligns with your topics.
- **Source count** -- How many original sources the Scout referenced when generating this idea.
- **Date** -- When the idea was created, shown as a relative timestamp (e.g., "2 hours ago").

When viewing ideas from all publications, each card also displays the publication name so you can tell which blog it belongs to.

## Quick actions

You don't always need to open an idea to act on it. When you hover over an idea card, a **Dismiss** button appears on the right side. Click it to quickly remove the idea from your active pipeline without leaving the list. This is handy for clearing out ideas that clearly don't fit.

The Dismiss button only appears on ideas that are in the "New" or "Reviewed" state -- you can't dismiss an idea that has already been promoted.

## Opening an idea

Click any idea card to navigate to its full detail page, where you can read the complete summary, explore source links, and take further actions like promoting the idea to a writing session. See [Idea Actions](/content-scout/idea-actions) for details.

## Auto-refresh

If the Content Scout finishes a run while you have the Ideas page open, the list automatically refreshes to include any newly generated ideas. You don't need to manually reload the page -- new ideas simply appear at the top of the list.

## Next steps

- [Learn about idea actions](/content-scout/idea-actions) to understand how to review, promote, or dismiss ideas from the detail page.
- [Read how the Scout works](/content-scout/how-it-works) if you want to understand what happens behind the scenes.
Understand how the Content Scout finds and generates content ideas for you.

# How It Works

The Content Scout is an AI-powered agent that continuously monitors the topics you care about and surfaces fresh content ideas for your publications. Instead of spending hours scanning blogs, news feeds, and industry sites for inspiration, the Scout does it for you -- so you can focus on writing.

## What the Scout does

When you add topics to a publication, the Content Scout goes to work. It scans sources across the web that are relevant to your topics, analyzes what it finds, and uses AI to generate unique content angles you can turn into posts.

The Scout runs on a schedule in the background, but you can also trigger it manually whenever you want a fresh batch of ideas.

## What you get for each idea

Every idea the Scout generates includes:

- **Title** -- A ready-to-use headline for the post.
- **Angle / Hook** -- The specific angle or unique take that makes this idea stand out.
- **Summary** -- A detailed overview of what the post could cover and why it matters.
- **Relevance score** -- A percentage score indicating how closely the idea matches your topics and audience.
- **Source links** -- The original articles, posts, or pages that inspired the idea, so you can reference them while writing.

## The ideas flow

Here is how ideas move through your content pipeline:

1. **Scout generates ideas** -- The Scout discovers new content opportunities and adds them to your Ideas page with a "New" status.
2. **You review** -- Browse the ideas, read the details, and decide which ones are worth pursuing.
3. **Promote or dismiss** -- Promote the ideas you like into full writing sessions, or dismiss the ones that don't fit your plans.

Once you promote an idea, Hot Metal creates a new writing session pre-loaded with the idea's title, summary, and sources. The AI writing partner picks up from there and starts drafting based on that context.

## Automated drafting

If you have set a publication's publish mode to "auto," the Scout can take things a step further. In auto mode, the Scout not only generates ideas but also kicks off the drafting process automatically -- so you come back to find draft posts waiting for your review rather than raw ideas.

## Next steps

- [Browse and filter your ideas](/content-scout/browsing-ideas) to find the best ones quickly.
- [Learn about idea actions](/content-scout/idea-actions) to understand how to promote ideas into writing sessions.
Offer RSS and Atom feeds so readers can subscribe to your publication.

# RSS & Atom Feeds

Hot Metal can generate RSS and Atom feeds for your publication, letting readers subscribe using their favorite feed reader. You control which feed types are available and whether they include full content or just excerpts.

## Enabling feeds

Feeds are configured in your publication's **Settings** page under the **RSS Feeds** section. There are two independent toggles:

- **Partial content feed** -- Generates feeds that include the post title and an excerpt.
- **Full content feed** -- Generates feeds that include the complete post content.

You can enable both, one, or neither. Each toggle saves immediately when you change it.

## Feed URLs

When feeds are enabled, your publication provides the following URLs:

### Partial content feeds

| Format | URL |
|--------|-----|
| RSS    | `https://yourslug.hotmetalapp.com/rss` |
| Atom   | `https://yourslug.hotmetalapp.com/atom` |

### Full content feeds

| Format | URL |
|--------|-----|
| RSS    | `https://yourslug.hotmetalapp.com/rss/full` |
| Atom   | `https://yourslug.hotmetalapp.com/atom/full` |

Replace `yourslug` with your publication's actual slug. You can find and copy these URLs directly from the publication settings page, where they're displayed as clickable links.

The feed URLs are also accessible from the **Feeds** dropdown button on your publication's home page in the dashboard.

## Partial vs. full feeds

### Partial feeds

Partial feeds include only the post title and a short excerpt. This encourages readers to click through to your blog to read the full article, which can be beneficial if you want to drive traffic to your site or if you rely on on-site analytics.

### Full feeds

Full feeds include the entire post content directly in the feed. Readers can consume your posts entirely within their feed reader without visiting your site. This is more convenient for readers and is preferred by many RSS enthusiasts.

Offering both options lets your readers choose the experience they prefer.

## Automatic regeneration

You don't need to manually update your feeds. Hot Metal automatically regenerates all enabled feeds whenever you publish a new post or update an existing one. Your subscribers will see new content appear in their feed readers shortly after publication.

## Sharing your feed URLs

If you want to make it easy for readers to find your feeds, consider:

- Adding a link to your RSS or Atom feed in your blog's footer or sidebar (if your template supports it).
- Sharing the feed URL on your social media profiles.
- Including the feed URL in your newsletter or welcome email.

Most modern feed readers auto-discover feeds from a site's HTML, but providing a direct link ensures readers can always find and subscribe to your publication.
Manage reader comments on your publication.

# Comments

Hot Metal includes a built-in commenting system that lets readers leave feedback on your published posts. You control whether comments are enabled, how they're moderated, and can manage them from a dedicated page in the dashboard.

## Enabling comments

Comments are disabled by default. To turn them on:

1. Open your publication and go to **Settings**.
2. Scroll to the **Comments** section.
3. Check the **Enable comments** checkbox.

Once enabled, a comment form will appear at the bottom of every published post on your blog. You can disable comments at any time by unchecking the same box -- existing comments will be hidden from the blog but not deleted.

## Moderation modes

When comments are enabled, you choose how new comments are handled:

### Auto-approve

Comments appear on your blog immediately after passing automated spam and content filters. This is the default mode and works well if you want a low-friction experience for readers.

### Require approval (pre-approve)

Comments are held in a "pending" state and don't appear on your blog until you manually approve them. This gives you full control over what shows up on your site, and is recommended if you want to review every comment before it's published.

You can switch between modes at any time from the Comments section of your publication settings. The change takes effect immediately for all new comments.

## The Comments page

To manage comments for a publication, navigate to the **Comments** page. You can get there from:

- The publication home page (click **View all** next to Recent Comments).
- The publication settings page (click the **Comments page** link in the Comments section).

### Filtering comments

The Comments page has three filter tabs at the top:

- **All** -- Shows all comments that haven't been deleted.
- **Pending** -- Shows only comments waiting for your review. A badge shows the count of pending comments.
- **Approved** -- Shows only comments that have been approved and are visible on your blog.

### Approving comments

When using pre-approve moderation, pending comments show a checkmark button. Click it to approve the comment -- it will immediately become visible on your blog.

### Deleting comments

Every comment (pending or approved) has a trash icon button. Clicking it removes the comment. Deleted comments no longer appear on your blog or in the "All" filter.

### Comment details

Each comment in the list shows:

- The **author's name** and email (if provided).
- The **post** the comment was left on (shown as the post slug).
- A **timestamp** showing when the comment was submitted.
- The **status** badge (pending or approved).
- A **preview** of the comment content.

## Spam protection

Hot Metal uses Cloudflare Turnstile to protect your comment forms from spam and bot submissions. Turnstile runs automatically in the background when readers submit a comment -- there's no CAPTCHA puzzle for your readers to solve. Comments that fail the Turnstile challenge are silently rejected.

## Email notifications

When a new comment is submitted on one of your posts, Hot Metal sends you an email notification so you can review it promptly. This is especially useful when you're using pre-approve moderation and want to stay on top of incoming comments.

Set up a new publication with the guided wizard.

# Creating a Publication

A publication is your blog or content channel in Hot Metal. It's the foundation for everything else -- topics, ideas, writing sessions, and published posts all live under a publication. You can create one in about a minute using the guided wizard.

## Opening the wizard

From the **Publications** page, click the **New Publication** button. If you don't have any publications yet, you'll see a welcome screen with a prompt to create your first one.

The wizard walks you through five steps. A progress bar at the top shows where you are, and you can navigate back to previous steps at any time.

## Step 1: Basics

Enter the foundational details for your publication:

- **Name** -- The display name for your blog (e.g., "Looking Ahead").
- **Slug** -- A URL-friendly identifier that becomes your subdomain. For example, a slug of `looking-ahead` gives you the URL `looking-ahead.hotmetalapp.com`. The slug auto-generates from your name, but you can customize it.
- **Description** -- A brief summary of what the publication covers. This helps the Content Scout and AI writer understand the focus of your blog.

The slug can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Once your publication is created, the slug determines your live URL, so choose something memorable.

## Step 2: Topics

Topics tell the Content Scout what subjects to monitor for content ideas. Enter a topic name and an optional description to guide the scout's focus.

For example, you might add:

- **AI in Software Engineering** -- "Focus on practical applications of LLMs in code review, testing, and developer tooling."
- **US Politics & Policy** -- "Focus on policy analysis and legislative impacts rather than partisan commentary."

You can add multiple topics here, or skip this step and add them later from your publication settings. The Content Scout needs at least one topic to find ideas for you.

**Tier limits apply:** Creator plans allow up to 3 topics per publication. Growth and Enterprise plans allow unlimited topics.

## Step 3: Writing Style

Choose the tone and voice the AI writer uses when drafting posts for this publication. You have three options:

- **Default style** -- A balanced, professional tone that works well for most publications.
- **Prebuilt styles** -- Select from Hot Metal's built-in style options, each with a distinct personality and voice.
- **Set my own tone** -- Describe exactly how you want the AI to write in your own words (e.g., "Conversational and witty, like explaining tech to a smart friend over coffee").

This step is optional -- you can skip it and configure writing styles later from the Styles page.

## Step 4: Publish Mode

Choose how much automation you want for your publication:

- **Gather Ideas** -- The Content Scout finds ideas based on your topics. You decide what to write and when to publish. Best if you want full manual control.
- **Draft Mode** (recommended) -- The Content Scout finds ideas and the AI writes drafts for you. You review everything and decide what to publish. A good balance of automation and editorial control.
- **Auto Publish** -- The Content Scout finds ideas, writes posts, and publishes them automatically on your schedule. Best for established publications with clear topic boundaries.

When you select Draft Mode or Auto Publish, you can also set a **posts per week** target. This tells the system how many drafts or published posts to aim for each week. Tier limits apply -- Creator plans allow up to 3 posts per week, Growth plans allow up to 10.

## Step 5: All Set

The final step shows a summary of everything you configured: your topics, writing style, and publish mode. From here you have two options to get started:

- **Find me ideas now** -- Triggers the Content Scout immediately so it starts searching for ideas based on your topics.
- **Write my first post** -- Opens a new AI writing session so you can start drafting your first post right away.

Either option takes you out of the wizard and into the main app. Your publication is already created at this point -- you can always come back to adjust settings later.

## Publication limits by tier

The number of publications you can create depends on your subscription tier:

| Tier       | Publications |
|------------|--------------|
| Creator    | 2            |
| Growth     | 5            |
| Enterprise | Unlimited    |

If you've reached your limit, you'll need to upgrade your plan or delete an existing publication before creating a new one.
Control when and how often the Content Scout runs.

# Scout Scheduling

The Content Scout runs on a schedule you define, automatically searching for ideas based on your publication's topics. You control how often it runs, what time zone it operates in, and what it does with the ideas it finds.

## Accessing the schedule

Open your publication's **Settings** page and scroll to the schedule section. You'll see a summary of your current schedule and publish mode. Click **Edit** to open the full schedule editor.

## Publish mode

Before configuring the schedule, choose how the Content Scout should handle the ideas it finds:

- **Gather Ideas** -- The scout finds ideas and adds them to your Ideas page. You decide what to write and when to publish.
- **Draft** -- The scout finds ideas and the AI automatically writes draft posts. You review and decide what to publish.
- **Auto Publish** -- The scout finds ideas, writes posts, and publishes them on your cadence without manual intervention.

You can change the publish mode at any time. When you select Draft or Auto Publish, you'll also set a **posts per week** target that tells the system how many drafts or published posts to aim for each week.

Posts-per-week limits depend on your tier:

| Tier       | Max posts per week |
|------------|-------------------|
| Creator    | 3                 |
| Growth     | 10                |
| Enterprise | Unlimited         |

## Schedule types

The schedule editor offers three ways to control how often the Content Scout runs:

### Daily

The scout runs once per day at a specific hour you choose. This is the default and works well for most publications.

Select the hour from the dropdown -- times are displayed in 12-hour format (e.g., "8:00 AM", "2:00 PM").

### Multiple times per day

The scout runs evenly spaced throughout the day. You choose how many times per day (2 through 6), and the system calculates the intervals automatically.

For example, selecting 3 times per day runs the scout at approximately 12:00 AM, 8:00 AM, and 4:00 PM.

**This option is available on the Growth plan and above.** Creator plan users will see it listed but grayed out, with a prompt to upgrade.

### Every N days

The scout runs at a specific hour, but only every few days instead of daily. You choose both the interval (every 2 to 7 days) and the time of day.

This is a good option if your publication covers a niche where news doesn't move as quickly and you don't need daily scouting.

## Timezone

All schedule times are based on the timezone you select. Hot Metal supports a curated list of major timezones across the Americas, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. Your browser's timezone is selected by default.

## Next run preview

The schedule editor shows a **Next scout** preview that displays the exact date and time of the next scheduled run, formatted in your selected timezone. This helps you confirm that your schedule is set up the way you expect.

## Run Scout Now

If you don't want to wait for the next scheduled run, you can trigger the Content Scout manually. Click the **Run Scout Now** button to start an immediate search for ideas.

The button is only available when your publication has at least one topic. If you haven't added any topics yet, you'll see a message prompting you to add one first.

## Saving your schedule

After making changes in the schedule editor, click **Save Schedule** to apply them. If you change your mind, click **Cancel** to discard your changes and return to the summary view. The publish mode is saved immediately when you change it, but schedule timing changes (timezone, frequency, hour) require clicking Save.
Configure your publication's name, author, template, feeds, and more.

# Publication Settings

Every publication has a settings page where you can fine-tune how it looks, how the AI writes for it, and what features are enabled. To get there, open a publication and click the **Settings** button.

Settings auto-save as you type -- there's no save button to click for most fields. Changes to text fields (name, description, author, writing tone) save automatically after a short pause.

## General settings

### Name

The display name of your publication. This appears in the Hot Metal dashboard and as the title on your published blog.

### Description

A summary of what your publication covers. This helps the Content Scout understand your blog's focus when searching for ideas, and helps the AI writer stay on-topic when drafting posts.

### Default Author

The author name that appears on published posts. You can override this on individual posts, but the default author saves you from entering it every time.

### Writing Tone

A free-text field where you can describe the tone and voice you want the AI writer to use. For example: "Skeptical tech analyst. Conversational but data-driven." This works alongside (or instead of) a writing style selection.

### Writing Style

Select a predefined writing style from the dropdown. You can choose from built-in styles or any custom styles you've created on the Styles page. Selecting "None (use default)" gives you a balanced, professional tone. A link to **Manage Styles** lets you jump directly to the Styles page to create or edit custom styles.

### Publication Template

Choose the visual design for your published blog. Hot Metal includes three templates:

- **Starter** -- Clean, minimal, content-focused design.
- **Editorial** -- Magazine-style with serif typography and drop caps.
- **Bold** -- Tech-forward with geometric layout and thick borders.

Template changes take effect on your live blog immediately.

## RSS Feeds

Control whether your publication offers RSS and Atom feeds for subscribers. See the [RSS & Atom Feeds](/publications/rss-feeds) guide for full details on feed URLs and formats.

- **Partial content feed** -- Includes the post title and an excerpt. Encourages readers to click through to your site.
- **Full content feed** -- Includes the complete post content in the feed itself.

You can enable both, either, or neither. Feed URLs are displayed below the toggles when at least one feed type is enabled.

## Comments

Enable or disable reader comments on your posts. When comments are enabled, you can choose a moderation mode:

- **Auto-approve** -- Comments appear on your blog immediately after passing spam and content filters.
- **Require approval** -- Comments are held for your review before appearing on the site.

For more details on managing comments, see the [Comments](/publications/comments) guide.

## Danger Zone

At the bottom of the settings page, you'll find the option to **delete the publication**. This permanently removes the publication along with all its topics and ideas. You'll be asked to confirm before anything is deleted.

Deleting a publication cannot be undone, so make sure you're certain before proceeding.
Create and manage API keys for programmatic access.

# API Keys

API keys let external tools, AI agents, and custom integrations access your Hot Metal account programmatically. Each key acts as a Bearer token for the Hot Metal API.

## Creating a key

1. Go to **Settings** and scroll down to the **API Keys** section.
2. Click **Create new API key**.
3. Optionally, give the key a descriptive label (for example, "My AI agent" or "CI/CD pipeline"). This helps you remember what each key is used for.
4. Click **Create key**.

Your new key will appear in a highlighted banner at the top of the section. It starts with `hm_` followed by a random string.

**Copy the key immediately.** For security reasons, the full key is only shown once. After you dismiss the banner or navigate away, you will only see a masked version (e.g., `hm_****abcd`).

## Using a key

Include your API key as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header of your HTTP requests:

```
Authorization: Bearer hm_your_key_here
```

See the [API Reference](/api) docs for the full list of available endpoints.

## Viewing your keys

The API Keys section lists all of your active keys. For each key, you can see:

- **Label** -- The name you gave it (or "Untitled key" if you did not set one).
- **Masked key** -- The last four characters of the key, so you can identify it.
- **Created date** -- When the key was generated.
- **Last used** -- When the key was last used to make an API call (or "Never used" if it has not been used yet).

## Revoking a key

If you no longer need a key, or if you suspect it has been compromised:

1. Find the key in the list and click **Revoke**.
2. A confirmation modal will appear, showing the key label and last four characters so you can verify you are revoking the right one.
3. Click **Revoke key** to confirm.

Revocation is permanent and takes effect immediately. Any agent or integration using that key will lose access as soon as the key is revoked. You can always create a new key if needed.

## Best practices

- **Use one key per integration.** If you have multiple tools accessing your account, create a separate key for each one. That way you can revoke a single key without disrupting everything else.
- **Label your keys clearly.** A good label makes it easy to audit which keys are in use and what they are for.
- **Revoke unused keys.** If a key shows "Never used" and you do not remember creating it, it is safe to revoke.
- **Never share keys in public repositories or logs.** Treat API keys like passwords.
Define what your Content Scout should look for.

# Topics

Topics are the keywords and subjects that tell Hot Metal's Content Scout what to search for. Each publication has its own set of topics, and the scout uses them to find relevant news, trends, and inspiration that can become blog posts.

## How topics work

When you add a topic to your publication, you're giving the Content Scout a clear signal about what kind of content to find. Each topic has:

- **Name** -- The subject area (e.g., "AI in Software Engineering", "Climate Policy", "Indie Game Development").
- **Description** (optional) -- Additional guidance that helps the scout focus. For example: "Focus on practical applications of LLMs in code review, testing, and developer tooling rather than theoretical AI research."
- **Priority** -- How much weight the scout gives to this topic when searching for ideas.

The description is especially useful when a topic name is broad. It tells the scout which angle or subtopics matter most to you.

## Adding a topic

1. Open your publication and go to the **Settings** page.
2. Scroll to the **Topics** section and click **Add Topic**.
3. Enter a name and optional description.
4. Select a priority level.
5. Click **Create**.

The new topic appears in your topic list immediately. The Content Scout will start using it on its next scheduled run.

## Editing a topic

Click on any topic in the list to open the edit dialog. You can change the name, description, and priority. Click **Update** to save your changes.

## Priority levels

Each topic has a priority that influences how the Content Scout ranks and selects ideas:

| Priority | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| **Normal** | Standard weight. The scout treats this topic equally with other Normal topics. |
| **High** | The scout gives this topic more attention and is more likely to surface ideas related to it. |
| **Urgent** | Top priority. The scout actively prioritizes this topic above all others. Use this sparingly for time-sensitive or critical subjects. |

Priority badges appear next to each topic in the list so you can see the weighting at a glance.

## Activating and deactivating topics

Each topic has a toggle switch in the topic list. Deactivating a topic tells the Content Scout to temporarily stop searching for ideas related to it, without deleting the topic entirely. This is useful when you want to pause coverage of a subject but plan to come back to it later.

Click the toggle to switch a topic between active and inactive. Inactive topics appear dimmed in the list.

## Deleting a topic

Click the trash icon next to a topic to delete it. You'll be asked to confirm before the topic is removed. Deleting a topic is permanent and cannot be undone.

## Tier limits

The number of topics you can add to a single publication depends on your subscription tier:

| Tier       | Topics per publication |
|------------|----------------------|
| Creator    | 3                    |
| Growth     | Unlimited            |
| Enterprise | Unlimited            |

If you've reached your topic limit, you'll see a message letting you know. You can upgrade your plan to add more topics, or remove an existing topic to make room.
Your home base for managing publications, ideas, and writing sessions.

# Dashboard

The Dashboard is the first thing you see when you sign in to Hot Metal. It gives you an at-a-glance overview of your publications, recent content ideas, and active writing sessions -- everything you need to pick up right where you left off.

## Dashboard layout

Your Dashboard is organized into several sections, each designed to surface what matters most.

### Getting Started checklist

If you're new to Hot Metal, you'll see the **Getting Started checklist** at the top of the Dashboard. This is a step-by-step guide that walks you through setting up your first publication, generating ideas, and publishing your first post. A progress bar tracks how far along you are.

Once you've finished the checklist (or dismissed it), it's replaced by **Quick Actions** -- a set of shortcut buttons that let you jump straight into common tasks.

For full details on the checklist, see [Onboarding Checklist](/getting-started/onboarding-checklist).

### Quick Actions

After the Getting Started checklist is dismissed, the top of the Dashboard shows Quick Actions -- large, clickable cards for the things you'll do most often:

- **Start Writing** -- Opens a new AI writing session. If you have multiple publications, you'll pick which one to write for.
- **Get New Ideas** -- Triggers the Content Scout to find fresh content ideas for your publication. If you have multiple publications, you'll choose which one to search for.
- **Create Writing Style** -- Takes you to the Styles page to define a custom writing voice. This action appears until you've created your first custom style.

### Publications

The **Publications** section displays cards for each of your publications. Click any card to jump into that publication's management page, where you can view its posts, topics, and settings.

A **View all** link takes you to the full Publications page.

### Latest Ideas

The **Latest Ideas** section shows your most recent content ideas from the Content Scout. Each idea displays:

- The idea title
- Which publication it belongs to
- How recently it was created
- A status badge (such as *new*, *reviewed*, or *promoted*)

Click any idea to view its full details. Use the **View all** link to browse all your ideas on the Ideas page.

### Active Writing Sessions

The **Active Sessions** section lists your in-progress writing sessions. Each session card shows:

- The session title (or "Untitled session" if you haven't named it yet)
- When it was last updated
- The current draft version number (for example, *v2*, *v3*)

Click any session card to jump back into the writing experience. The **View all** link takes you to the Writing page where you can see all sessions.

## Navigating from the Dashboard

Every section on the Dashboard includes a **View all** link that takes you to the corresponding full page. You can also use the sidebar navigation at any time to move between Dashboard, Publications, Ideas, Writing, Styles, and Settings.

The Dashboard is always just a click away from the sidebar when you want to return to your overview.
A guided walkthrough to set up your first publication and publish your first post.

# Onboarding Checklist

When you first sign in to Hot Metal, the Dashboard displays a **Getting Started checklist** at the top of the page. This checklist walks you through the complete journey from creating your first publication to publishing your first post. A progress bar shows how many steps you've completed, and each step unlocks the next.

## The six steps

### 1. Create your first publication

Your first publication is created through the **Publication Wizard**, a guided setup flow that walks you through naming your publication, describing its focus, and configuring the basics. Once this step is complete, it's automatically checked off in the checklist.

A publication is the foundation of everything in Hot Metal -- your ideas, writing sessions, and published posts all belong to a publication.

### 2. Add topics to your publication

Topics tell the Content Scout what subjects to look for when generating content ideas. Head to your publication's settings page (the checklist provides a direct **Add topics** link) and add at least one topic that reflects what your publication covers.

The more specific your topics are, the better the Content Scout's suggestions will be. For example, "React performance optimization" will generate more targeted ideas than just "React."

### 3. Run the Content Scout

Once you have topics set up, it's time to let the AI go to work. Click the **Run now** button directly in the checklist to trigger the Content Scout. It will search for trending content and discussions related to your topics and generate a batch of fresh post ideas.

You'll see a confirmation message when the scout starts running, and new ideas will appear on your Ideas page shortly after.

### 4. Review an idea

Head over to the Ideas page (the checklist gives you a **Go to ideas** link) and review one of the ideas the Content Scout generated. You can read through the idea details and either mark it as reviewed or promote it to signal that you want to write about it.

Reviewing ideas helps you curate the best suggestions and keeps your content pipeline focused.

### 5. Write your first post

Now for the fun part. Click **Start writing** in the checklist to create your first writing session. This opens the AI writing experience, where you collaborate with an AI writing partner through conversation to draft your post. Describe what you want to write, provide direction, and the AI will help you shape a complete draft.

You can go through multiple draft versions, refining and iterating until you're happy with the result.

### 6. Publish your post

The final step is to publish. When your post is ready, use the **Publish** action to push it live to your blog. This is the finish line -- once you've published, you've completed the entire Getting Started journey.

## Progress tracking

The checklist includes a visual progress bar that fills as you complete each step. Next to the checklist title, you'll see a counter (for example, *3/6*) showing how many steps you've finished.

Steps are checked off automatically based on your activity -- you don't need to manually mark them as done. The checklist detects when you've added topics, when the Content Scout has generated ideas, when you've reviewed an idea, and so on.

## Completing or dismissing the checklist

When all six steps are complete, the checklist shows a congratulatory "You're all set!" message with a **Dismiss checklist** button.

If you'd prefer to explore on your own, you can dismiss the checklist at any time by clicking the close button in the top-right corner. A confirmation dialog will ask if you're sure, since dismissing the checklist is permanent. Don't worry -- all the features mentioned in the checklist are always accessible from the sidebar navigation.

Once the checklist is dismissed, it's replaced by **Quick Actions** on the Dashboard, giving you shortcut buttons to start writing, get new ideas, and create a custom writing style. You can learn more about these in the [Dashboard](/getting-started/dashboard) guide.
Understand Hot Metal's subscription plans and manage your billing.

# Plans & Billing

Hot Metal offers three plans so you can pick the level that fits your content workflow. Start free, and upgrade whenever you need more capacity.

## Plans overview

### Creator (free)

The Creator plan is free forever -- no credit card required. It includes everything you need to start publishing:

- **2 publications**
- **3 topics** per publication
- **3 auto-published posts** per week
- Built-in (prebuilt) writing styles
- Unlimited AI writing sessions
- Unlimited social publishing
- AI image generation
- All templates
- RSS/Atom feeds
- Reader comments

### Growth (paid)

The Growth plan is for creators who are serious about growing their audience consistently. It includes everything in Creator, plus:

- **5 publications**
- **Unlimited topics** per publication
- **10 auto-published posts** per week
- All Content Scout schedules, including multiple times per day
- Up to **5 custom writing styles**
- Priority support

Growth is available on a monthly or yearly billing cycle. The yearly option gives you a discount compared to paying month-by-month.

### Enterprise (custom pricing)

Enterprise is built for teams and organizations with advanced needs. It includes everything in Growth, plus:

- Unlimited publications
- Unlimited posts per week
- Unlimited writing styles
- Custom domain (planned)
- Team collaboration (planned)
- Custom templates (planned)
- API access (planned)
- SSO (planned)
- Dedicated support and SLA

To learn more about Enterprise pricing, contact us at **hello@hotmetalapp.com**.

## Upgrading to Growth

You can upgrade from Creator to Growth in two ways:

1. **From Settings** -- Go to **Settings > Plan & Billing** and click **Upgrade to Growth**.
2. **From an upgrade prompt** -- Whenever you hit a Creator-tier limit (for example, trying to create a fourth topic), Hot Metal will show an upgrade prompt. Click **Upgrade** to continue.

Either path opens the pricing view, where you can toggle between **Monthly** and **Yearly** billing. When you're ready, click **Start Growth Plan** and complete checkout through Paddle. Your upgrade takes effect immediately.

## Managing your billing

Once you have an active Growth subscription, go to **Settings > Plan & Billing**. You will see your current plan name, status badge, and next billing date.

Click **Manage Billing** to open the Paddle customer portal, where you can:

- Update your payment method
- View and download invoices
- Change your billing address

Paddle handles all billing, invoicing, and tax compliance. It accepts major credit and debit cards, PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and various local payment methods depending on your region.

## Plan status badges

Your plan card in Settings displays a status badge so you always know where things stand:

| Badge | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Active** | Your subscription is current and in good standing. |
| **Trial** | You are on a free trial of the Growth plan. |
| **Canceled** | You canceled, but you still have access until the end of your current billing period. The badge shows the exact date your access ends. |
| **Past Due** | A payment failed. Update your payment method in the Paddle portal to avoid interruption. |
| **Paused** | Your subscription is paused. Resume it from the Paddle portal. |

## Canceling your subscription

If you decide Growth is not for you, go to **Settings > Plan & Billing** and click **Cancel Plan**. A confirmation modal will explain what happens next:

- Your subscription stays active until the end of the current billing period.
- After that, your account reverts to the free Creator plan.
- Your content is never deleted, but some features may be limited by the Creator-tier caps (for example, only 3 topics per publication).

Click **Cancel Plan** in the modal to confirm, or **Keep Plan** if you change your mind.

It discovers trending topics in your niche, generates content ideas, drafts posts through a conversational AI writing partner, and publishes finished pieces to your blog and social media channels -- all from one place.

## Create your account

To get started, head to the Hot Metal sign-up page and create an account. You can sign up with your email address, or use your existing Google or GitHub account for one-click registration.

Once you've signed up, you'll land on the **Dashboard** -- your home base for everything in Hot Metal.

## Your first visit

If this is your first time signing in, the Dashboard will greet you by name and present the **Getting Started checklist**. This guided walkthrough takes you from zero to your first published post in six steps. We recommend following it in order -- each step builds on the previous one.

You can learn more about the checklist in the [Onboarding Checklist](/getting-started/onboarding-checklist) guide.

## Navigating Hot Metal

The sidebar gives you access to every section of the platform:

- **Dashboard** -- Your home base. See your publications, latest ideas, and active writing sessions at a glance.
- **Publications** -- Create and manage your blogs. Each publication has its own topics, ideas, and published posts.
- **Ideas** -- Browse content ideas generated by the AI-powered Content Scout. Review, promote, or dismiss ideas as you see fit.

Ready to dive in? Your first step is to create a publication -- that's the foundation everything else builds on.

# Social Connections

Hot Metal can publish directly to your social media accounts when you publish a blog post. Before you can use this feature, you need to connect at least one social account.

## Supported platforms

Hot Metal currently supports two platforms:

- **LinkedIn** -- Share posts directly to your LinkedIn profile.
- **X (Twitter)** -- Post a tweet with a link to your published blog post.

You can connect one account per platform.

## Connecting an account

1. Go to **Settings** and scroll down to the **Connections** section.
2. Find the platform you want to connect (LinkedIn or X).
3. Click **Connect**.
4. You will be redirected to the platform's authorization page. Sign in if prompted, then approve access for Hot Metal.
5. After you authorize, you will be redirected back to Settings. A success message confirms the connection.

Once connected, you will see:

- **LinkedIn**: Your connected account name and token expiry information.
- **X**: Your connected @username and token expiry information.

A green dot next to the platform name indicates an active connection.

## Disconnecting an account

1. Go to **Settings > Connections**.
2. Click **Disconnect** next to the account you want to remove.
3. A confirmation modal will appear. Click **Disconnect** to confirm.

After disconnecting, you will not be able to publish to that platform until you reconnect.

## Token refresh

OAuth tokens refresh automatically in the background, so you generally do not need to worry about them expiring. The token expiry information shown on each connection card is for informational purposes -- if a token is about to expire and cannot be refreshed, Hot Metal will prompt you to reconnect.

## Using social connections when publishing

Once your accounts are connected, the Publish modal will let you choose which platforms to post to when you publish a blog post. If no accounts are connected, the social publishing options will not be available.

For details on the publishing workflow, see the [Publish to Blog](/publishing/publish-to-blog) guide.
Control which email notifications you receive.

# Notifications

Hot Metal sends email notifications to keep you informed about important events -- new content ideas, drafts that are ready, published posts, and reader comments. You can turn each notification type on or off individually.

## Managing your preferences

1. Go to **Settings** and scroll down to the **Notifications** section.
2. You will see a toggle switch for each notification type.
3. Click a toggle to turn that notification on or off. Changes save immediately.

## Notification types

| Notification | What it means |
|---|---|
| **New ideas found** | The Content Scout has discovered new content ideas for one of your publications. |
| **Draft ready for review** | An auto-generated draft has been written and is waiting for you to review it. |
| **Post auto-published** | A post has been automatically published to your blog. |
| **New comment** | A reader left a comment on one of your published posts. |

## Defaults

All notification types are enabled by default when you create your account. You can disable any of them at any time.

## Delivery

All notifications are delivered by email to the address associated with your Hot Metal account. There are no in-app notifications at this time.
Converse with the AI writer to shape your content.

# Chat Panel

The chat panel is the left side of the workspace. It's a conversational interface where you collaborate with the AI writer to shape your blog post. Instead of filling out forms or editing a blank document, you simply describe what you want in plain language and the AI does the heavy lifting.

## How the chat works

The chat works like a messaging conversation. You type a message, the AI responds, and the back-and-forth continues until your draft is where you want it. The AI isn't just generating text in the chat -- it's actively reading and updating your draft behind the scenes using tools like `save_draft` and `get_current_draft`.

When you open a brand-new session, the chat starts with a prompt: "Describe your blog post idea, and the AI writer will help you draft it." From there, you lead the direction.

## What you can ask

You can steer the AI in many ways throughout the conversation. Here are some examples:

- **Start from scratch** -- "Write an intro about the future of remote work."
- **Adjust tone** -- "Make the opening paragraph more casual and conversational."
- **Add content** -- "Add a section about the security implications."
- **Edit existing content** -- "Shorten the conclusion to two sentences."
- **Ask questions** -- "What angle do you think would work best for a developer audience?"
- **Request structure changes** -- "Break the third section into bullet points instead of paragraphs."

There's no rigid format -- just write naturally and the AI will follow your lead.

## Writing phases

A typical session flows through a few natural phases:

1. **Interviewing** -- The AI may ask you clarifying questions about your topic, audience, and goals before it starts writing. This helps it produce a more targeted first draft.
2. **Drafting** -- Once it has enough context, the AI writes a full draft and saves it to the draft panel. You'll see the draft appear (or update) on the right side of the workspace.
3. **Revising** -- After the first draft, you can go back and forth with the AI to refine, expand, restructure, or polish the content. Each major revision creates a new draft version.

## Stop button

While the AI is generating a response, a stop button appears in the input area. Click it to cancel the current generation if you want to change direction or if the AI is going off track. The conversation preserves everything up to the point you stopped.

## Thinking indicator

When the AI is processing your message but hasn't started responding yet, you'll see a "Thinking..." indicator at the bottom of the chat. This lets you know it's working on your request.

## Auto-send for promoted ideas

If you created this session by promoting an idea from the Content Scout, the chat automatically sends an opening message on your behalf. This message tells the AI about the promoted idea and asks it to review the research context and begin outlining key points. You don't need to type anything -- the conversation kicks off immediately.

## Next steps

- [Learn about the Draft Editor](/writing/draft-editor) to understand how drafts are displayed and how to edit them directly.
- [Explore Images](/writing/images) to learn how to generate featured images and add inline images to your post.
View, edit, and manage draft versions of your post.

# Draft Editor

The draft panel is the right side of the workspace. It shows the current state of your blog post and gives you tools to view, edit, copy, and publish your content.

## Viewing your draft

When the AI saves a draft during the conversation, it appears in the draft panel as formatted content -- headings, paragraphs, lists, code blocks, and images are all rendered so you can see what the final post will look like.

If no draft exists yet, you'll see a message: "No drafts yet. Chat with the AI to generate your first draft."

## Switching to edit mode

By default, the draft panel is in **view mode** -- a read-only, formatted display of your content. To make direct changes, click the **Edit** button (pencil icon) in the toolbar at the top of the panel.

In edit mode, you get:

- **An editable title field** at the top of the draft where you can type or change the post title.
- **A rich text editor** powered by Tiptap, where you can modify the body content directly.
- **A floating toolbar** that appears when you select text, with formatting options for bold, italic, code, and strikethrough.
- **An image button** in the toolbar above the editor for inserting inline images (more on this in the [Images](/writing/images) guide).

To switch back to view mode, click the **View** button (eye icon). Any pending changes are saved automatically before the switch.

## Auto-save

While you're editing, your changes are saved automatically after a brief pause (2 seconds of inactivity). You don't need to click a save button.

A save status indicator appears next to the edit toggle:

- **Unsaved** -- You have changes that haven't been saved yet.
- **Saving...** -- A save is in progress.
- **Saved** -- Your changes were saved successfully.

If the AI creates a new draft while you're editing, your pending changes are flushed and saved first, then the editor exits edit mode to show the new AI-generated version.

## Draft versioning

Every time the AI produces a new revision of your draft, it's saved as a separate **version**. This means you never lose previous iterations of your post.

A **version selector** dropdown appears in the header of the draft panel, showing all available versions (e.g. "Draft v1," "Draft v2," "Draft v3"). Each version also shows the draft title if one was set.

- The selector always jumps to the latest version when the AI creates a new one.
- You can select any previous version to review what the draft looked like at that point.
- Switching versions while in edit mode automatically saves your current changes and exits edit mode first.

## Publishing any version

You aren't limited to publishing only the latest draft. You can select any version from the version dropdown and click **Publish** to publish that particular revision. This is useful if the AI went in a direction you didn't love in a later version and you want to go back to an earlier one.

If a post has already been published from this session, the button changes to **Publish Again**, and a green check icon appears to indicate that a published version exists.

## Copying your draft

Click the **copy** icon in the toolbar to copy the full draft (title and content) to your clipboard as Markdown. This is handy if you want to paste it into another tool or share it outside Hot Metal.

## Next steps

- [Learn about Images](/writing/images) to generate featured images and upload inline images.
- [Explore Writing Styles](/writing/writing-styles) to customize the AI writer's voice and tone.
Generate AI featured images and upload inline images.

# Images

Hot Metal gives you two ways to add visuals to your posts: AI-generated featured images and inline image uploads. Both are built into the draft panel so you can handle images without leaving the workspace.

## Featured image generation

A featured image is the hero image that represents your post -- it typically appears at the top of the published article and in social media previews. Hot Metal can generate one for you using AI.

### Opening the image generator

In view mode, you'll see a **Generate Featured Image** link just below the draft header. Click it to expand the image generation section. (If you haven't created a draft yet, this button is disabled -- write some content first so the AI has context to work with.)

### Writing a prompt

The image generator shows a text area where you describe the image you want. You have two options:

- **Write your own prompt** -- Type a description of the image you have in mind, such as "A minimalist illustration of a developer working at a desk with multiple screens, warm lighting, flat design."
- **Auto-generate a prompt** -- Click the **Auto-generate prompt** button and the AI will analyze your draft content and create an image prompt for you. You can then edit the generated prompt before proceeding.

### Generating and selecting

Once you have a prompt, click **Generate 4 Options**. The AI will produce four image variations based on your description. While images are being generated, you'll see animated placeholder cards.

When the results are ready:

1. Click on the image you like best. A highlighted border and check icon confirm your selection.
2. Click **Use Selected** to set it as your featured image.

If none of the options are quite right, you can:

- Click **Edit Prompt** to revise your description and try again.
- Click **Regenerate** to get four new options with the same prompt.

### After selecting

Once you select a featured image, it appears as a thumbnail in the draft header. You can click **Change** at any time to open the generator again and pick a different image. The selected featured image is included when you publish your post.

## Inline image uploads

In addition to the featured image, you can insert images directly into the body of your draft. These inline images appear within the text and are preserved when you publish.

### How to add inline images

You need to be in **edit mode** (click the pencil icon) to insert inline images. There are three ways to add them:

- **Toolbar button** -- Click the **Add Image** button in the editing toolbar above the draft. This opens a file picker where you can choose an image from your computer.
- **Paste from clipboard** -- Copy an image and paste it directly into the editor. The upload happens automatically.
- **Drag and drop** -- Drag an image file from your desktop or file manager and drop it into the editor.

### Supported formats and limits

- **Formats** -- JPEG, PNG, WebP, and GIF.
- **Size limit** -- 5 MB per image. If you try to upload a larger file, you'll see an error message.

### Upload behavior

When you add an image, it's uploaded to Hot Metal's storage and inserted at your cursor position. While the upload is in progress, the **Add Image** button shows "Uploading..." and is temporarily disabled. Once the upload completes, the image appears inline in your draft and is saved as part of the content.

Inline images are preserved through all draft versions and are included when you publish.

Your split-screen environment for AI-assisted writing.

# The Workspace

The workspace is where the actual writing happens. When you open a session, you land in a split-screen environment designed to keep the conversation with the AI and the evolving draft visible at the same time.

## Two-panel layout

The workspace is divided into two panels:

- **Chat panel (left)** -- A conversational interface where you talk to the AI writer. Describe what you want, ask for changes, or give feedback -- the AI uses this conversation to shape your draft.
- **Draft panel (right)** -- A live view of your current draft. As the AI writes or revises content during the conversation, the draft updates here automatically. You can also switch to edit mode and make changes directly.

This side-by-side design means you never have to switch tabs or copy-paste between a chat window and a document. Everything stays in view.

## Resizable divider

A vertical divider sits between the two panels. You can drag it left or right to give more space to whichever panel you need at the moment -- for example, widening the draft panel when you're reviewing a long post, or expanding the chat when you're having a detailed back-and-forth.

The divider can be moved anywhere between 30% and 70% of the screen width. Your preferred position is saved in your browser, so it stays where you left it the next time you open a session.

You can also use the keyboard to adjust the divider. Focus it with Tab, then press the **Left** or **Right** arrow keys to nudge it in 5% increments.

## Mobile layout

On smaller screens (under 768px wide), the workspace switches to a tabbed layout instead of side-by-side panels. Two tabs appear at the top of the screen:

- **Chat** -- Shows the chat panel.
- **Draft** -- Shows the draft panel.

Tap a tab to switch between them. This keeps the workspace fully usable on phones and tablets without cramming two panels into a narrow viewport.

## How chat and draft work together

The chat and draft panels are connected in real time. When the AI saves a new version of your draft during the conversation, the draft panel refreshes automatically to show the latest content. You don't need to reload or manually sync anything -- just keep chatting, and the draft keeps up.

Create and manage AI-assisted writing sessions.

# Writing Sessions

A writing session is your workspace for creating a single blog post with the help of an AI writing partner. Each session pairs a conversational chat interface with a live draft editor, so you can go from a rough idea to a polished post without leaving the page.

## Creating a session

To start a new session, click the **New Session** button on the Writing page. A dialog will appear with the following options:

- **Title** (optional) -- Give your session a name so you can find it later. If you leave this blank, it will appear as "Untitled session" in your list.
- **Publication** -- If you have more than one publication, choose which one this session is for. Linking a session to a publication automatically applies that publication's writing style and settings. If you only have one publication, it is selected for you automatically.

Click **Create** and you'll be taken straight into the workspace to start writing.

> Sessions can also be created automatically when you promote an idea from the Content Scout or click **Use** on a writing style card. In both cases, the session is pre-configured with the relevant context.

## The session list

The Writing page shows all of your sessions in a card grid. Each card displays:

- **Title** -- The name you gave the session, or "Untitled session" if you skipped it.
- **Status badge** -- Shows whether the session is Active, Completed, or Archived.
- **Last updated** -- A relative timestamp (e.g. "2 hours ago") so you can see which sessions have recent activity.
- **Version indicator** -- If the session has at least one draft, a version number (e.g. "v3") appears in the bottom-right corner of the card. This tells you how many draft revisions the AI has produced so far.

Click any card to open that session's workspace.

## Deleting a session

To delete a session, hover over its card and click the trash icon that appears. You'll be asked to confirm before anything is removed. Deleting a session is permanent -- all chat history, drafts, and generated images associated with it will be lost.

Update posts that are already live on your publication.

# Editing Published Posts

Published a post and need to make changes? Hot Metal lets you edit any published post and push updates live. The editing workflow preserves your original content and gives you the full power of the AI writing partner to revise.

## Finding your published posts

You can access your published posts in two ways:

- **Publication home page** -- Navigate to your publication from the sidebar and you will see your published posts listed there.
- **Published Posts page** -- From your publication's home page, open the Published Posts page to see a dedicated list of all posts published under that publication. Each post shows its title, author, and when it was published.

## Starting an edit

Click the **Edit** button (pencil icon) on any published post. Hot Metal creates a new writing session pre-loaded with the post's original content. The Markdown from your original draft is preserved exactly as it was, so you pick up right where you left off.

You are taken directly into the writing workspace, where you can:

- Edit the draft directly in the editor.
- Use the AI chat to revise, expand, or rewrite sections.
- Generate a new featured image if needed.

## Publishing your update

When you are ready to push your changes live, click the **Publish** button to open the Publish modal. Because this is an update to an existing post, you will notice a few differences from the first-time publish flow:

- The modal title shows **Update Post** instead of "Publish Post."
- The **slug is read-only** -- you cannot change the URL of an existing post. This ensures your links and SEO remain intact.
- **SEO fields are pre-filled** -- The excerpt, hook, tags, and author fields are populated from the existing published post. You can edit any of them before updating.

Click **Update** to push the changes live. Your post is updated in place on your publication's site -- the same URL, now with your revised content.

## Social sharing on update

You can also share the updated post on LinkedIn and X (Twitter) when you update. The social sections in the Publish modal work the same way as they do for a first-time publish -- toggle on the platforms you want, customize the text, and everything goes out together.

Customize the AI writer's voice and tone.

# Writing Styles

Writing styles control how the AI writer sounds. They define the voice, tone, vocabulary, and personality that the AI uses when drafting your posts. Instead of getting generic, one-size-fits-all output, you can make the AI match your unique writing voice -- or create entirely different voices for different publications.

## What a writing style includes

Each writing style is built from a **system prompt** -- a set of detailed instructions that tell the AI how to write. When a style is created from a URL analysis (more on that below), it also includes structured details about:

- **Voice characteristics** -- Person (first, second, third), formality level, and personality traits.
- **Sentence structure** -- Notable sentence patterns and tendencies.
- **Vocabulary preferences** -- Vocabulary level, favorite phrases, power words, and jargon usage.
- **Rhetorical devices** -- Techniques like analogies, rhetorical questions, or repetition.
- **Content approach** -- How the writer uses examples, data, storytelling, and humor.
- **Do's and don'ts** -- Specific guidelines for what the AI should and shouldn't do.

All of this feeds into the AI writer's instructions so it produces content that feels like *you* wrote it.

## Built-in styles

Hot Metal comes with prebuilt writing styles that are ready to use immediately:

- **Conversational Expert** -- A knowledgeable but approachable voice that explains complex topics in a friendly, accessible way.
- **Professional Analyst** -- A more formal, data-driven voice suited for industry analysis and thought leadership.

Built-in styles can't be edited directly, but you can **duplicate** any of them to create a copy that you can then customize however you like.

## Creating custom styles

You can create custom styles in two ways:

### Write a prompt manually

Click **Create Style** on the Styles page and fill in the form:

- **Name** -- A short label for the style (e.g. "My Blog Voice").
- **Description** -- A brief summary of what the style sounds like.
- **System prompt** -- Detailed instructions for the AI writer. Describe the tone, vocabulary, sentence structure, and any patterns to follow or avoid. This is the core of the style -- the more specific you are, the better the results.

### Learn from a URL

If you already have a blog with an established voice, Hot Metal can analyze it and generate a style automatically. In the Create Style modal:

1. Switch to the **Learn from URL** tab.
2. Paste the URL of a blog (e.g. `https://example.com/blog`).
3. Click **Analyze**.

The AI reads the blog's content, studies the writing patterns, and generates a complete style profile -- including the system prompt and all the structured fields listed above. This process can take a few minutes depending on the site.

Once the analysis is complete, you're taken to the prompt tab where you can review and tweak the generated system prompt before saving. The style name is auto-filled based on the blog's domain, but you can change it to whatever you prefer.

## Assigning styles to sessions

There are several ways a writing style gets applied to a session:

- **Publication default** -- When you set a writing style on a publication, every session linked to that publication uses it automatically.
- **Per-session override** -- You can choose a specific style when creating a session, overriding whatever the publication default is.
- **From the Styles page** -- Click the **Use** button on any style card to create a new writing session with that style applied immediately.

### Style cascade

The AI writer resolves which style to use in this order:

1. **Session style** -- If the session was created with a specific style, that wins.
2. **Publication style** -- If no session-level style is set, the publication's default style is used.
3. **System default** -- If neither is set, the AI falls back to its built-in writing behavior.

## Editing and managing styles

On the Styles page, your custom styles appear under **My Styles**. For each style you can:

- **Edit** -- Update the name, description, or system prompt.
- **Duplicate** -- Create a copy of the style that you can modify independently.
- **Delete** -- Permanently remove the style. You'll be asked to confirm first.

Built-in styles can be duplicated but not edited or deleted.

## Plan limits

The number of custom styles you can create depends on your subscription tier:

- **Creator** -- Built-in styles only. Custom styles are not available on this plan.
- **Growth** -- Up to 5 custom writing styles, plus full access to the URL analysis feature.

If you hit your limit, you'll see an upgrade prompt suggesting you move to a higher plan. Your usage count is displayed alongside the "My Styles" heading so you always know where you stand.

Share your posts on X with AI-crafted tweets.

# X (Twitter)

Hot Metal can post a tweet to your X account when you publish a blog post. The AI generates a tweet from your draft, you can edit it to your liking, and it goes out alongside your blog publish.

## Prerequisites

Before you can share to X, you need to connect your X account. Go to **Settings > Social Connections** and follow the prompts to authorize Hot Metal. Once connected, the X section in the Publish modal becomes active.

If your account is not connected, you will see a "Connect in Settings" note next to the X option.

## Enabling X sharing

In the Publish modal, you will see the **X** card. Click the card or check the checkbox to enable X sharing. The card expands to show the tweet editor.

## AI-generated tweet

As soon as you toggle X sharing on, Hot Metal automatically generates a tweet from your draft using AI. You will see a loading indicator while the AI works. The generated tweet is designed to be engaging and concise, drawing from your draft's hook and key points.

An **AI** badge appears next to the "Tweet text" label when the text was generated by AI.

## Editing your tweet

You can edit the tweet text freely in the text area. Once you make manual changes, the AI will not overwrite your edits. The text area is yours to customize however you want.

If you want a fresh AI-generated tweet after making edits, you can toggle X sharing off and back on to trigger a new generation.

## Character counter

A character counter appears in the top-right corner of the text area showing your current count out of the 280-character maximum. The counter accounts for X's t.co link shortening -- all URLs count as 23 characters regardless of their actual length. The count also includes the blog post link that Hot Metal automatically appends to your tweet.

If you exceed 280 characters, the counter turns red and a warning appears. You will need to shorten the text before publishing.

## Blog link

Hot Metal automatically appends a link to your published blog post at the end of the tweet. You do not need to add it yourself -- the link is included when the tweet is sent. A note below the text area reminds you: "Link to your blog post will be included."

## How publishing works

When you click **Publish** (or **Update**), your tweet is sent alongside your blog publish. The modal shows you the result of each destination after publishing. You will see a success message like "Posted on X" in the confirmation banner, or an error message if something went wrong.
